info.title: Agent-Native Middleware API
info.description:
## Agent-Native Middleware API

Headless infrastructure for the B2A (Business-to-Agent) economy. This API provides a broad set of services that give AI agents the 'hands' and 'eyes' to manipulate the digital and physical world.

### Capability Surface

- **IoT Protocol Bridge** (`/v1/iot`) — Secure, ACL-enforced protocol translation for IoT devices (MQTT, CoAP, Zigbee, etc.)
- **Autonomous Product Manager** (`/v1/telemetry`) — Ingest telemetry, detect anomalies, auto-generate pull requests to fix bugs
- **Programmatic Media Engine** (`/v1/media`) — Video ingestion, viral hook detection, reframing, captioning, and cross-platform distribution
- **Agent Communications** (`/v1/comms`) — Agent registration, structured messaging, capability discovery, and swarm task handoffs
- **Extended Agent Operations** — Content factory, oracle discovery, billing, MCP tools, security scanning, launch automation, protocol generation, RTaaS, sandboxes, telemetry scopes, dashboards, broadcast, AI helpers, webhooks, KYC, API keys, AWI, discovery, well-known, and static documentation routes

### Authentication

All endpoints require an API key passed via the `X-API-Key` header.

### For Agents

This API is designed for programmatic consumption. See `/llm.txt` for LLM-optimized documentation and `/openapi.json` for the full spec.

### v0.2.0

✅ Now MCP-native with discoverable monetized tools, dry-run sandboxing, KYC gating, and automatic key rotation.

post.description: Register a device for protocol bridging. The device will receive a unified REST endpoint that translates HTTP requests into the device's native protocol. Topic ACLs are enforced on every message. An empty ACL dict defaults to deny-all for maximum security.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

get.description: Paginated listing of all devices registered to your API key.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

get.description: Retrieve registration details and bridge endpoint for a specific device.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

delete.description: Remove a device from the bridge. All pending messages will be dropped.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

post.summary: Send a message to a device
post.description: Send a message through the protocol bridge to the device's native protocol. The topic must match an allowed ACL pattern. Messages to denied topics (e.g., camera feeds) will be rejected with a 403.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

post.description: Subscribe to messages from a device topic. Returns a webhook URL or WebSocket endpoint that agents can poll for incoming data. Topic must have READ permission in the device's ACL.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

post.description: Submit a batch of telemetry events for anomaly detection. Events are processed asynchronously. The Autonomous PM will analyze patterns across events to identify error spikes, latency regressions, and missing feature patterns.
Convenience endpoint for submitting one event at a time.

get.description: Retrieve anomalies detected by the Autonomous PM. Anomalies are classified by severity and category, with optional LLM-generated fix suggestions and auto-PR links.
Retrieve detailed information about a specific anomaly, including suggested fixes.

post.description: Instruct the Autonomous PM to generate a code fix for the given anomaly and optionally push it as a pull request. Use dry_run=true to preview the proposed diff without committing.

get.description: Aggregate statistics across all ingested telemetry events. Useful for agents monitoring system health at a glance.

post.description: Submit a video for the media engine pipeline. Provide either a source_url for the engine to fetch, or use the returned upload_url to push the file directly. Processing begins automatically after upload.

get.description: Check the current status of a video in the processing pipeline.

Retrieve the viral hooks detected in a processed video. Hooks are ranked by confidence_score. Each hook identifies a high-engagement moment based on speech patterns, emotional peaks, visual surprises, or audience retention signals.

schema.title: Response Get Viral Hooks V1 Media Videos  Video Id  Hooks Get

post.description: Generate reframed, captioned clips from detected viral hooks. Clips are automatically reframed to the target aspect ratio(s) and overlaid with animated captions in the chosen style. 80%% of social video is consumed muted — captions are not optional.

Push generated clips directly to social platforms via their APIs. No app is opened. Supports YouTube Shorts, TikTok, Instagram Reels, X Video, and LinkedIn Video. Set optimize_schedule=true to let the algorithm pick optimal posting windows based on historical engagement data.

get.description: Retrieve metadata and download URL for a generated clip.

post.description: Register a new agent in the communication network. The agent receives a unique ID and API key for messaging. Capabilities are used by other agents to discover and hand off tasks.

get.description: List all agents in the communication network, optionally filtered by capability.

post.summary: Send a message to another agent
post.description: Send a structured message to a registered agent. Messages are routed based on priority: critical messages are delivered immediately with aggressive retry, low priority messages are batched. If the recipient has a webhook, delivery is push-based; otherwise the recipient must poll.

get.description: Poll an agent's inbox for pending messages. Messages are marked as delivered upon retrieval. Use this if the agent does not have a webhook configured.

post.description: Confirm receipt and processing of a message.

Find an agent with the required capability and hand off work to it. This is the core swarm intelligence mechanism: instead of one 'God model' doing everything, tasks are routed to specialist agents. Returns the handoff message and target agent details.

Submit a source asset and target formats. The factory will generate multiple content pieces adapted to each format: short videos, static images, text posts, audiograms, carousels, blog excerpts, and email snippets. Processing is async.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Check the status of a content generation pipeline.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Retrieve all content pieces generated by a pipeline.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Retrieve metadata and download URL for a specific content piece.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Retrieve all live content campaigns.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: The 'Big Red Button' — submit a source video URL and targeted hooks. Each hook is a specific segment (e.g., 30-45s reaction clip, 60s explainer). The factory applies the 1-to-N multiplication rule: each hook produces multiple format-adapted pieces (short videos, quote cards, carousels, text posts) all rendered in 9:16 vertical with animated captions. Then the AlgorithmicScheduler staggers posts across platforms (TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels) to maximize view velocity.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Check the status and results of a live content campaign.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Feed engagement data from platform APIs into the scheduling engine. The scheduler uses this data to learn optimal posting windows. More data = smarter scheduling = higher view velocity.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: View summary of ingested engagement data.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Generate an algorithmically-optimized posting schedule for content pieces. The scheduler analyzes historical engagement data per platform to pick the highest-engagement time slots. Posts are spread across days to avoid audience fatigue (configurable via max_posts_per_day).

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Returns all historical scan reports, newest first.

post.description: Deploy the security swarm to attack specified services with selected attack categories. Returns immediately with scan ID; the swarm runs asynchronously. Default: full scan of all services with all vectors.

get.description: Returns the full security scan report including all discovered vulnerabilities, severity breakdown, security score, and prioritized remediation recommendations.
Returns just the vulnerabilities from a scan, optionally filtered by severity. Machine-readable format for the Autonomous PM to auto-generate fix PRs.

post.description: Runs a fast, surface-level scan focused on CRITICAL and HIGH severity vectors only (ACL bypass, auth probes, privilege escalation). Returns the full report synchronously — use for CI/CD gates.

post.summary: Crawl and index an external API
post.description: Submit a URL for the Oracle to crawl. It will attempt to discover the API's capabilities via /.well-known/agent.json, /llm.txt, or OpenAPI specs, then compute a compatibility score indicating how well it integrates with our middleware.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Submit multiple URLs for concurrent crawling and indexing.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Browse the Oracle's index of external APIs. Filter by compatibility tier or directory type. Sorted by compatibility score (highest first).

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Retrieve full details for a specific indexed API.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Push our API profile into external agent directories and registries. This is how agents find us — by being listed in the directories they already crawl. Supports /.well-known, MCP server listings, plugin stores, and centralized agent registries.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: View all directories where our API has been registered.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Compute our API's overall visibility across agent networks. Includes registration count, discovery hit rate, compatibility distribution, and actionable recommendations.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Returns the agent network graph centered on our API. Shows all indexed APIs, registered directories, and their compatibility relationships. Use this to visualize our position in the agent ecosystem.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Called when an external agent discovers our API. Tracks referrer for analytics. This endpoint should be called by our middleware whenever an agent first hits / or /.well-known/agent.json.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Create a human-owned root account that acts as the 'liability sink' for agent spending. Sponsors ingest fiat currency via payment rails and convert it to ecosystem credits. Agent wallets are provisioned from sponsor balances.

Create a pre-paid wallet for an autonomous agent, funded from a sponsor's balance. The agent can then transact autonomously up to its budget without human intervention. Supports daily spend limits and automatic refills.

Create a spend-capped child wallet from a parent agent's balance. Enables hierarchical swarm budgeting: a master agent building a complex tool can spin up specialized sub-agents (code-writer, tester, deployer), each with a micro-budget and hard lifetime cap. Supports TTL for auto-expiry.

post.summary: Reclaim unspent credits from a child wallet
post.description: Close a child wallet and return unspent credits to the parent. Use this when a sub-agent completes its task or you want to reallocate budget.

get.description: Hierarchical budget summary for an agent's child wallets.

post.summary: Charge a wallet for API usage
Prepare a fiat top-up via Stripe
post.description: Create a Stripe PaymentIntent for fiat payment. After payment succeeds, credits are minted automatically via webhook. Requires KYC verification if enabled for the wallet.
Transfer credits from one wallet to another (agent-to-agent handoff).

parameters.name: correlation_id

schema.description: Optional ID to link related transfers
schema.title: Correlation Id

parameters.description: Optional ID to link related transfers

post.description: Register a new service in the marketplace that agents can discover and pay for.

get.description: List all registered billable services in the marketplace.
Get current spend velocity metrics for a wallet.

post.description:
Create a new dry-run session for simulating billing operations. All charges simulated within this session use a virtual balance derived from the wallet's real balance minus simulated charges. Sessions expire after 15 minutes.

Use this to:
- Test if a multi-step workflow fits within your budget
- Estimate total cost before committing to execution
- Plan complex agent workflows safely

get.description: Retrieve current state of a dry-run session.

delete.description: End a dry-run session and get a summary of all simulated charges. This does NOT affect the real wallet - it's purely informational.

post.description: Commit all simulated charges to real billing. This applies all sandbox charges to the wallet's real balance. Use this after reviewing the simulation results and deciding to proceed.
Revert a sandbox session and discard all simulated charges. No changes are made to the real wallet. This is useful when the simulation shows the operation would fail or you're not ready to proceed.

Simulate a charge without affecting real balance or triggering velocity monitoring. Returns cost estimate and virtual balance impact.

Options:
- Use session_id for multi-step simulation (tracks cumulative cost)
- Omit session_id for single-shot estimation

The ignition key. Executes all four launch phases in sequence:

1. **FUND** — Create sponsor wallet, inject seed capital, provision agent wallets
2. **INFILTRATE** — Crawl 6 major APIs, register in 4 agent directories
3. **IGNITE** — Launch B2A content campaign (3 hooks × N formats = 40+ pieces)
4. **ARM** — Activate Red Team perimeter scanning across all API paths

Returns a LaunchResponse (the system's birth certificate) with full metrics.

Runs a comprehensive validation sweep before Day 1 launch:

1. **KEYS** — Reject test-key/placeholder API keys, validate Stripe tokens
2. **DOMAIN** — Verify BASE_URL is a real domain, manifests will resolve
3. **ORACLE** — Validate agent directory registration URLs
4. **ASSETS** — Check content source URLs and crawl targets

Returns a GO/NO-GO verdict with per-check details. Fix all critical failures before calling POST /v1/launch.

post.summary: Meta-Launch: Spawn the Genesis Builder Agent
post.description:
The ultimate proof of infrastructure autonomy. Executes a 6-phase lifecycle:

1. **FUND** — Create sponsor wallet, spawn Genesis Builder child wallet with spend cap
2. **BUILD** — Genesis Agent writes a micro-service API
3. **SECURE** — RTaaS attacks the new service (thorough intensity)
4. **TEST** — Sandbox validates the agent's generalization ability
5. **PUBLISH** — Protocol Engine generates llm.txt + OpenAPI + agent.json
6. **MONITOR** — Telemetry Scope pipeline watches the new service

Returns a GenesisReport — proof that the infrastructure can self-replicate.

get.description: Returns historical launch reports (birth certificates).

post.summary: Generate protocol package from source code
post.description:
Submit raw API source code and receive a complete agent-discoverable package:

1. **llm.txt** — LLM-optimized documentation
2. **OpenAPI 3.1 spec** — Machine-readable API schema
3. **agent.json** — Agent discovery manifest
4. **Oracle registration** — (optional) Push to agent directories

The instant go-to-market engine for any tool an agent builds.

get.description: Retrieve historical generation runs.
Retrieve the full output of a protocol generation run.

post.summary: Create a Red Team scanning job
post.description: Submit external endpoint URLs for penetration testing. Our Red Team swarm will attack the specified targets and return a structured vulnerability report with CWE mappings and remediation steps. Use this before deploying any agent-built tool to production.

get.description: View all RTaaS jobs, optionally filtered by tenant.
Retrieve the full vulnerability report for an RTaaS job.
Retrieve just the vulnerability list with remediation steps.

post.description:
Spin up a headless puzzle environment. The agent-under-test must discover hidden rules through interaction, not instruction.

**Environment Types:**
- **pattern** — Discover input→output transformation rules
- **navigation** — Navigate a state graph to reach a goal
- **api_mock** — Interact with a shifting mock API
- **adversarial** — Environment actively tries to deceive

post.summary: Submit an action to the environment
post.description: The agent-under-test submits an action, receives feedback and reward. No instructions are given — the agent must discover the rules.

Compute the final generalization score. Call this after the agent has finished interacting with the environment. Score components:

- **Efficiency** (0-20): How quickly did the agent solve it?
- **Solved bonus** (0-50): Did the agent actually solve it?
- **Score bonus** (0-30): Cumulative reward from actions

get.description: Retrieve current state of a sandbox environment.

post.description: Create an isolated sandbox environment for tool testing.

get.description: Get the current state of a sandbox environment.

delete.description: Destroy a sandbox environment and release resources.

post.description: Execute a tool within an isolated sandbox environment.
Execute a named tool in a specific environment.

Create an isolated telemetry pipeline for monitoring an agent-built tool. Events ingested into this pipeline are analyzed independently, with per-tenant anomaly detection and optional auto-PR generation.
Route telemetry events from your tool into its scoped pipeline. Anomaly detection runs automatically on each batch.

get.description: Retrieve all anomalies detected in a telemetry pipeline.

post.description: Auto-generate a pull request to fix a detected anomaly. Targets the git repo configured on the pipeline.

get.description: Aggregated telemetry stats: error rates, latencies, anomaly counts.

Returns the complete real-time state of all 15 pillars:
economics, security posture, telemetry health, sandbox scores, protocol coverage, and Genesis launch history.
Credit metrics + full wallet tree (Sponsor → Agent → Child).
Aggregate vulnerability metrics across all RTaaS jobs.
Pipeline health, error rates, latencies, anomaly counts.
All Genesis Builder Agent lifecycle reports.

post.description: Pushes discovery artifacts (llm.txt, OpenAPI, agent.json) to all registered agent directories. The network effects engine.

get.description: Real-time discovery tracking: impressions, lookups, integrations.

post.description: Simulate an impression, lookup, or integration event.

get.description: All agent directories we can broadcast to.

post.description:
Make an autonomous decision based on context.

The AI analyzes the situation and recommends the best action.

get.description: Get recent decisions for an agent.

schema.title: Response Get Decisions V1 Ai Decisions  Agent Id  Get

post.description:
Automatically diagnose and suggest a fix for an issue.

The AI analyzes error logs and produces a potential fix.
Note: Fixes are not auto-applied — human review recommended.

get.description: Get a specific self-heal result.

post.description:
Ask questions about the system in natural language.

The AI answers based on the provided data context or general knowledge.
Store a memory for an agent.
Recall memories for an agent.

Learn from an experience.

The AI extracts patterns and stores insights for future decisions.

get.description: Plain-text documentation optimized for LLM agents to understand this API.
Returns a JSON index of all available documentation resources. Agents use this to navigate the doc tree and find relevant sections without parsing the entire llm.txt.
Returns a standard agent plugin manifest for agent directories and plugin registries. This is how autonomous agents discover and evaluate this service.

post.description:
Receive and process Stripe webhook events.

Stripe will retry this endpoint if we return non-200.
Our service layer handles idempotency internally via
UNIQUE constraint on payment_intent_id + IntegrityError catch.

Events handled:
- payment_intent.succeeded → Mint credits to wallet
- payment_intent.payment_failed → Log and notify
- charge.refunded → Debit wallet
- identity.verification_session.verified → Approve KYC
- identity.verification_session.requires_input → Log
- identity.verification_session.redacted → Expire verification

Receive and process Stripe Identity webhook events.

These events are for KYC verification status updates.
Note: Stripe often sends these to the same /webhooks/stripe endpoint,
but we handle them separately here for clarity.

get.description:
Return the MCP tools.json manifest.

This is the standard MCP server discovery endpoint.
Agents should fetch this first to understand available tools.

Query Parameters:
    category: Optional service category filter

Returns:
    MCP tools.json manifest with tool definitions

MCP tools manifest at the standard .well-known location.

This follows the MCP specification for tool discovery.

post.description:
Handle MCP JSON-RPC messages.

Supports:
- tools/list: List available tools
- tools/call: Execute a tool

The request body is a JSON-RPC 2.0 request.

Invoke an MCP-enabled service.

This endpoint:
1. Verifies the API key
2. Routes the call through the billing layer
3. Executes the service function (local) or forwards to API (persistent)
4. Returns the result

For persistent services, see POST /v1/billing/services/{id}/invoke

get.description:
List all available MCP-enabled services with pagination.

Query Parameters:
    category: Optional service category filter
    limit: Maximum number of tools to return (default 100, max 500)
    offset: Number of tools to skip for pagination

Returns:
    Paginated list of tool definitions with schemas

schema.title: Response List Mcp Tools Mcp Tools Get

get.summary: Get a specific MCP tool definition
get.description:
Get the MCP tool definition for a specific service.

Returns the full tool schema including:
- inputSchema
- outputSchema (if available)
- pricing and category annotations

schema.title: Response Get Mcp Tool Mcp Tools  Service Id  Get

post.description:
Create a Stripe Identity verification session for a sponsor wallet. This returns a URL that the user should be redirected to for completing identity verification. After verification, the wallet's kyc_status will be updated via webhook.

Only sponsor wallets require KYC. Agent and child wallets inherit trust.

get.summary: Get KYC status for a wallet
get.description: Get the current KYC verification status for a wallet.
Get detailed information about a specific verification.

post.description: Create a new API key for a wallet. The key is only shown once - store it securely.

get.summary: List API keys for a wallet
get.description: Get all API keys for a wallet. Keys are masked for security.

post.description: Rotate an API key by creating a new one and optionally revoking the old one. Returns the new key which is only shown once.

delete.description: Immediately revoke an API key. This action cannot be undone.

post.description: Immediately revoke ALL API keys for a wallet. Use for security incidents.

get.description: Get audit logs for API key rotations on a wallet.

schema.title: Response Get Rotation Logs V1 Api Keys  Wallet Id  Logs Get

post.description: Pause all tasks globally (human intervention).

Create a WebAuthn challenge for high-risk AWI action verification.
Verify the WebAuthn credential response from the client.

Detach the Playwright DOM bridge from an AWI session.

Returns the session to mock/internal AWI mode. The browser context is closed.

get.description: Check if a session has a DOM bridge attached.

post.description: Create a browser session for bidirectional DOM translation.

delete.description: Destroy a Playwright bridge session and cleanup resources.

get.description: List all active DOM bridge sessions.

post.description: Execute an AWI action against real browser DOM via Playwright.

get.description: Get current state of the DOM as an AWI representation.

post.description: Preview what commands will be generated for an action.
Index a completed AWI session for future retrieval.
Semantic search over past AWI session memories.

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific memory.

delete.description: Delete a memory from the index.

get.description: Get relevant context from past sessions for the current session.
Get statistics about the memory store.
Get all indexed memories for a session.

delete.description: Delete all memories for a session.

get.description: Returns a comprehensive machine-readable manifest of all services, MCP tools, AWI endpoints, and pricing. This is the primary endpoint for autonomous agents to discover and integrate with this platform.
Returns all available MCP tools with their schemas and pricing.
Returns all Agentic Web Interface endpoints and the action vocabulary.
Returns the MCP tools manifest for tool discovery.
Returns a machine-readable manifest of all available services, their base paths, and links to documentation. This is the first endpoint an agent should hit to understand what this API offers.
Returns 200 if the API is running. Use for Kubernetes livenessProbe.
Returns 200 if all dependencies are ready. Use for Kubernetes readinessProbe.

Probes every external dependency (PostgreSQL, Redis, MQTT broker, Stripe, LLM provider) in parallel with a short timeout. Each entry reports status, latency_ms, and an error message when unreachable. Deps whose consumers are in simulation mode return `not_used` so the health verdict doesn't degrade on mock-only deployments.

LiveCampaignRequest.title: LiveCampaignRequest
LiveCampaignRequest.description:
Launch a full live content campaign from a single source.

This is the 'Big Red Button' — submit a source URL and a set of
targeted hooks. The factory will:
1. Extract each hook segment
2. Apply the 1-to-N multiplication rule per hook
3. Render all pieces in 9:16 vertical with animated captions
4. Auto-schedule across platforms via the AlgorithmicScheduler
